commit_message:
[dv/dpi] Fix assignment to SystemVerilog chandle

Section 6.14 of the standard states that assignment to chandle can only
be made from `null` or an other chandle, not from an integer literal.

This patch allows the compilation of the simulation models with modelsim

Signed-off-by: Tarik Graba <tarik.graba@telecom-paris.fr>
pull_body:
Section 6.14 of the standard states that assignment to chandle can only
be made from `null` or an other chandle, not from an integer literal.

This patch allows the compilation of the simulation models with modelsim
Issue title: [DPI] Out-of-spec chandle nullifier breaks parsing + fix
Issue Body: Hi OpenTitan team!     We can see in   - uartdpi.sv  - dmidpi.sv   - jtagdpi.sv    ```systemverilog    chandle ctx;    [... stuff ...]        ctx = 0;  ```    This is a syntax error and breaks parsing on compliant parsers (e.g. Verific).     Per the SystemVerilog spec (IEEE Standard for SystemVerilog 1800-2017) p. 106     > Only the following assignments can be made to a handle:  >   Assignment from another chandle  >   Assignment to null    The fix is simple:   ```systemverilog  ctx = null;  ```  on the relevant lines.
Comments: rswarbrick: As a note, if you want this to work with anything but super-recent Verilator versions, you have to do a hacky ifdef thing. See 1e3e5fb for the gory details. msfschaffner: @rswarbrick can you update this issue & close if this has already been addressed? rswarbrick: This was fixed by c43c58b (from PR #5491) last March.
